steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b=f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the values
steps.content: The slope is $-2$, so $m=-2$. The line passes through $(0,-19)$, so $f(0)=-19$ and $b=-19$. Therefore $$f(x)=-2x-19.$$

steps.title: Match the equation
steps.content: Choice A has slope $-19$, not $-2$. Choice B has the right slope but goes through $(0,0)$ instead of $(0,-19)$. Choice D has positive slope. Only choice C, $f(x)=-2x-19$, matches. Choice C is correct.
